Politics.headline: Republicans take another crack at Homeland Security funding, citing Iran war
Politics.detail: Republicans are using the war in Iran and the potential for retaliatory terrorist attacks to push for votes on a funding bill for the Department of Homeland Security, as funding lapsed on Feb. 14. Speaker Mike Johnson emphasizes the urgency of a fully funded DHS due to the military action in Iran.

Politics.headline: House to vote on Iran war powers resolution in a test of Trump's strategy
Politics.detail: The House is preparing to vote on a war powers resolution to halt President Donald Trump's attack on Iran, reflecting congressional unease over the widening conflict. The administration aims to destroy Iran's ballistic missiles and has indicated Israel was ready to act, with American bases facing retaliation if the U.S. did not strike first.

Politics.headline: Government responds to Backbench Business Committee 15th anniversary report
Politics.detail: The Government has published its response to the Backbench Business Committee's 15th anniversary report, which calls for reforms to ensure backbench business debates remain effective, relevant, and timely. The report celebrates the committee's anniversary and advocates for reforms to maintain the effectiveness of backbench business debates.

Politics.headline: Asylum seekers who break the law or work illegally to lose right to accommodation
Politics.detail: Asylum seekers who break the law, work illegally, or can financially support themselves will lose government-funded accommodation under new plans. Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood says only those with legitimate asylum claims who follow the rules will receive taxpayer-funded support as part of a major shake-up of the UK's immigration system.

Politics.headline: US politics live. House to vote on limiting Trump's powers in Iran war after Senate measure fails
Politics.detail: The House is set to vote on limiting President Trump's powers regarding the Iran war, following the Senate's failure to pass a similar measure. This action reflects ongoing debate and concern in Congress over the extent of presidential authority in the conflict with Iran.

Finance & Economy.headline: South Korea's stock market suffers biggest drop in history amid US-Iran war
Finance & Economy.detail: South Korea's stock market experienced its largest decline ever amidst the ongoing conflict between the US and Iran, reflecting investor anxiety and economic uncertainty in the region.

Finance & Economy.headline: IRGC says Iran in 'complete control' of Strait of Hormuz amid Trump threats
Finance & Economy.detail: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) asserted Iran's complete control over the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for oil transportation, amidst rising tensions and threats from the Trump administration.

Finance & Economy.headline: Gamblers make big profits on Iran strikes, raising insider trading concerns
Finance & Economy.detail: Anonymous bets placed hours before attacks on Iran, which resulted in the death of Supreme Leader Khamenei, have sparked worries about possible insider trading activities.

Finance & Economy.headline: FHFA chief says Trump deployed $200B to slash mortgage rates, impact was immediate
Finance & Economy.detail: The head of the Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) stated that the Trump administration implemented a $200 billion initiative to lower mortgage rates, which had an immediate effect on the market.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stocks rebound after strong economic updates and as oil prices stop spiking
Finance & Economy.detail: U.S. stocks are recovering following positive economic data and a stabilization in oil prices, providing some relief to investors after recent market volatility.

Science.headline: Scientists just looked inside Darwin's 200-year-old specimen jars without opening them
Science.detail: Scientists have used a laser technique to analyze Charles Darwin's original Galápagos specimens without opening their nearly 200-year-old jars, revealing the chemical makeup of the preservation fluids inside and offering new clues about historical preservation practices. The breakthrough could help museums protect millions of delicate specimens without risking damage.

Science.headline: Scientists discover the protein that malaria parasites can't live without
Science.detail: Researchers have identified a protein called Aurora-related kinase 1 (ARK1) that acts like a traffic controller during the malaria parasite's unusual cell division process, ensuring its genetic material is properly separated as it multiplies. When scientists switched off ARK1 in laboratory experiments, the parasite could no longer replicate correctly and failed to complete its life cycle in both humans and mosquitoes, effectively halting its ability to spread.

Science.headline: Science and policy experts unite to accelerate climate and health solutions
Science.detail: African universities and regional experts are working with Wellcome to establish new science and policy consortiums that will address the escalating public health crisis driven by climate change. These consortiums will focus on developing evidence-based solutions that reflect local priorities and translate rapidly into policy and practice, with an initial £40 million investment for consortiums in Southern and Western Africa.

Science.headline: Astronomers Spot a Record-Breaking 'Space Laser' 8 Billion Light-Years Away
Science.detail: Astronomers have detected the brightest 'cosmic beacon' ever observed, a mega-laser shooting at us from halfway across the universe. This record-breaking space laser is located 8 billion light-years away.

Science.headline: Cutting term of ERC president 'not essential,' Commission official says
Science.detail: The European Commission has softened its stance on halving the mandate of the president of the European Research Council (ERC) from four years to two, stating that it is open to adjusting this mandate. A senior advisor indicated that the proposal was more of a talking point and not an essential part of the Horizon Europe plan.

Health.headline: Africa CDC and Japan Institute for Health Security (JIHS) Sign Cooperation Agreement to Strengthen Global Health Security
Health.detail: The Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) and the Japan Institute for Health Security (JIHS) have signed a Memorandum of Cooperation (MOC) to strengthen bilateral collaboration in health security, infectious disease control, research, and public health capacity development. The agreement aims to address emerging public health threats and strengthen pandemic prevention and response.

Health.headline: The Pulse: UVa research highlights importance of mRNA vaccine work, Virginia joins multistate health coalition
Health.detail: An analysis from the University of Virginia warns that increased scrutiny and declining funding for RNA-based vaccine research could jeopardize decades of biomedical progress. The study, published in JAMA, examined grants related to RNA and found that funding also supported research into HIV, hepatitis and highly contagious tropical viruses.

Health.headline: Scientists Discover the Protein That Malaria Parasites Can't Live Without
Health.detail: Scientists have uncovered a crucial weakness in the malaria parasite that could open the door to new treatments. This discovery identifies a protein essential for the malaria parasite's survival, potentially paving the way for novel therapeutic interventions.

Health.headline: Why a proposed Colorado law may ban the practice of garnishing patients' paychecks to repay medical debt
Health.detail: A bill under consideration at the Colorado state capitol would prevent debt collectors from garnishing patients' paychecks for medical debt, making Colorado one of the few states to ban this practice. Critics say wage garnishment disproportionately affects lower-income patients, while opponents argue the ban could raise healthcare costs for everyone.

Health.headline: Ambulance services would get funding boost while saving Colorado millions under new bill
Health.detail: A bill in Colorado aims to increase funding for emergency medical services and save the state millions in healthcare spending by expanding the services EMS agencies can bill to Medicaid and private insurance. The bill, HB 26-1069, is projected to save the state millions annually by reducing emergency room and hospital visits.

Sports.headline: Harry Kane To Miss Bayern Munich Game Against Gladbach With Calf Knock
Sports.detail: Harry Kane will miss Bayern Munich's game against Gladbach due to a calf injury.

Sports.headline: Allen's record ton blasts New Zealand past South Africa into T20 World Cup final
Sports.detail: Finn Allen's record-breaking century propelled New Zealand to victory over South Africa in the T20 World Cup semi-final.

Sports.headline: NFL Free Agency: Needs for All 32 Teams
Sports.detail: A look at the biggest needs for all 32 NFL teams as they head into free agency.

Sports.headline: Premier League: Arsenal seizes control as Man City slips; Race for Top 5 tightens as Chelsea thrashes Villa and Man United loses
Sports.detail: Arsenal has taken the lead in the Premier League after Manchester City's recent slip-up, while the race for the top 5 intensifies.

Sports.headline: Raducanu hopes to rediscover 'natural' style that has been 'coached out of me'
Sports.detail: Emma Raducanu is aiming to get back to her original, more instinctive tennis style.

Entertainment.headline: Michelle Pfeiffer, 67, has social media buzzing about how she's 'still incredibly beautiful'
Entertainment.detail: Fans are expressing their admiration for Michelle Pfeiffer's enduring beauty, sparking a buzz on social media.

Entertainment.headline: Twisted Sister taps '80s metal icon to carry on legacy after frontman's sudden exit: 'You can't stop rock'
Entertainment.detail: Twisted Sister is bringing in a prominent figure from the 80s metal scene to continue their musical journey following the departure of their frontman.

Entertainment.headline: Lainey Wilson reveals Keith Urban's brutal 5-word reality check when she complained about grueling schedule
Entertainment.detail: Lainey Wilson shared that Keith Urban gave her a blunt five-word reminder when she expressed exhaustion from her demanding schedule.

Entertainment.headline: Nicole Kidman says she learned to perform autopsies for her latest role
Entertainment.detail: Nicole Kidman revealed that she learned how to perform autopsies in preparation for her most recent acting role.

Entertainment.headline: Nelson twins refused 'dishonorable' bankruptcy to protect father's legacy after inheriting millions in debt
Entertainment.detail: The Nelson twins chose to avoid bankruptcy to preserve their father's legacy, even after inheriting millions of dollars in debt.

Crime & Justice.headline: Springfield Man Sentenced to Over 12 Years in Prison for Federal Hate Crime Violation
Crime & Justice.detail: A Springfield man has been sentenced to over 12 years in prison for committing a federal hate crime.

Crime & Justice.headline: United States Leads Dismantlement of One of the World's Largest Hacker Forums
Crime & Justice.detail: The United States has led an international effort to dismantle one of the world's largest hacker forums.

Crime & Justice.headline: Justice Department Secures Settlement in Sexual Harassment Lawsuit Against Green Bay Landlord
Crime & Justice.detail: The Justice Department has reached a settlement in a sexual harassment lawsuit against a landlord in Green Bay.

Crime & Justice.headline: 65 victims identified, 60 arrests in year-long child sexual abuse operation in the Americas
Crime & Justice.detail: A year-long operation targeting child sexual abuse in the Americas has resulted in the identification of 65 victims and the arrest of 60 individuals.
Crime & Justice.source: United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime

Crime & Justice.headline: Bangladesh: Helping build the conditions for a credible election
Crime & Justice.detail: The UNODC is helping Bangladesh build conditions for a credible election.
Crime & Justice.source: United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime

Education.headline: Florida House, Senate find rare agreement on education bill
Education.detail: The Senate adopted the House version of a measure (HB 1201) that will require employees at all public schools, including charters, to get training in how to properly tend to students who are experiencing seizures. Schools also would be required to display posters, provided free of charge, that describe the basic steps for responding to an individual having a seizure.

Education.headline: Families turn to states for civil rights support as Trump dismantles the Education Department
Education.detail: With the federal Education Department's role in policing discrimination in schools waning due to mass firings and shifting priorities, states are stepping up to fill the gap. In Pennsylvania, a lawmaker is proposing a new state agency to investigate schools and uphold students' civil rights.

Education.headline: Education News India: Latest Board Exam, Live Results, Admission News, School & Colleges News
Education.detail: Delhi HC orders NTA to form expert panel on UGC NET History answer key 'errors', thousands of candidates wait for clarity. NEET UG 2026 Registration Ends Soon: Instructions to upload image, important deadlines and more.

Education.headline: Daily News 05 / 03 / 2026
Education.detail: The European Commission released guidelines to help teachers engage in the ethical use of AI and data in education, promote digital literacy, select high-quality digital education content, and teach informatics. The new guidelines on digital education content (DEC) introduce the EU's first-ever definition of DEC and offer clear criteria for identifying high-quality digital teaching materials.

Education.headline: WVU names its first enrollment VP after years of student losses
Education.detail: West Virginia University (WVU) has appointed its first enrollment VP after experiencing a decline in student population by nearly 5,000 students between 2016 and 2025.  WVU is preparing for a potential drop in college-aged individuals due to declining birth rates.

Lifestyle.headline: Passengers refusing to wear headphones on flights could be kicked off aircraft: 'It's about time'
Lifestyle.detail: A discussion has started about whether passengers should be forced to wear headphones on flights, and some people believe that those who don't should be kicked off the aircraft.

Lifestyle.headline: Chick-fil-A rolls back waffle fry recipe after fans blast change as 'terrible' and 'bland'
Lifestyle.detail: Chick-fil-A is reverting to its original waffle fry recipe after customers complained about a new version that was described as 'terrible' and 'bland'.

Lifestyle.headline: America's most overpriced pizza chain revealed, and it's not the one you think
Lifestyle.detail: A recent analysis has identified the most overpriced pizza chain in America, and it might not be the one you expect.

Lifestyle.headline: Hari Raya 2026: 10 Singapore and Malaysia labels to shop for your festive outfits
Lifestyle.detail: Planning your Raya 2026 looks? These labels offer everything from baju kurungs, kebayas, kaftans and versatile separates that balance modesty, comfort and style for the festivities.

Lifestyle.headline: Bromance, clams, gochujang: Celeb chefs Edward Lee, Ryu Soo-young explore Korean seafood in new road trip show
Lifestyle.detail: Celeb chefs Edward Lee and Ryu Soo-young explore coastal Korean cuisine in Ed & Ryu: Mad About Seafood, bonding, cooking and charming townspeople along the way.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Practical blueprint for growth and stability amid headwinds: China Daily editorial
Opinion/Editorial.detail: China's 2026 Government Work Report offers predictability and certainty in a world grappling with sluggish growth, geopolitical tension, and policy whiplash, setting a pragmatic GDP growth target of 4.5-5 percent for this year and presenting main objectives for the 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-30) period. The report emphasizes resilience, structural adjustment, risk control, and reform, with a focus on social equity and environmental metrics alongside economic ones.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: PPS wastefulness is holding Pittsburgh and its children back
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A new report reveals careless procurement practices within Pittsburgh Public Schools, highlighting a lack of structure and professionalism in handling expenses. The editorial argues that state intervention is necessary to address the district's compromised administration, governance, and culture, which resists change even in the face of crisis.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Denver's restaurants are still reeling
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A report indicates that Denver's restaurant industry faces "structural contraction, not recovery," with issues stemming from local factors such as high labor costs and minimum wage hikes. Despite menu prices increasing, earnings are down, and the editorial suggests that government intervention may not be the best solution to remedy the harm caused by previous government policies.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion | The Supreme Court is right. Parents get to decide how to raise their kids.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Supreme Court reinstated a lower court injunction blocking a California policy that required teachers to hide information from parents about a child's assumed name, pronouns, or identity at school unless the child consented to disclosure. The ruling reinforces the principle that government authority is secondary to parental rights, particularly in guiding the religious development of children.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL - Role model for objectification
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses Republic Act No. 11313, also known as the Bawal Bastos Law or Safe Spaces Act signed in April 2019, which penalizes various forms of harassment made in public spaces, workplaces, schools, and online. It appears to critique a situation related to the objectification of individuals, referencing the law as a framework.

World.headline: Iran launches new attacks, saying US will 'bitterly regret' sinking warship, calls for Trump's blood
World.detail: Iran launched a new wave of attacks at Israel, American bases and countries around the region, threatening that the United States would “bitterly regret” torpedoing an Iranian warship and calling for “Trump's blood.” Israel announced multiple incoming missile attacks, and Iranian state television said additional strikes also targeted U.S. bases.

World.headline: Middle East crisis live: Azerbaijan says drone attacks 'will not remain unanswered' as Iran denies involvement
World.detail: Azerbaijan accuses Iran of a drone attack on an airport that injured two people, and has stated that the attacks 'will not remain unanswered' as Iran denies involvement.

World.headline: China lowers economic growth target to 4.5-5% amid global, domestic uncertainties
World.detail: China has lowered its economic growth target to 4.5-5% amid global and domestic uncertainties.

World.headline: Commission holds first meeting of Special Panel on child safety online
World.detail: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen hosted the first meeting of the Special Panel on child safety online to provide expert recommendations to better protect and empower children online, exploring potential harmonised age restrictions to access social media.

World.headline: Second Iranian ship near Sri Lankan waters, Minister tells country's Parliament
World.detail: A second Iranian ship is reported to be near Sri Lankan waters.

Zimbabwe.headline: Another Zimbabwean killed in Russia-Ukraine war
Zimbabwe.detail: NewsDay reports that another Zimbabwean has died fighting in the Russia-Ukraine war, adding to the growing number of casualties among foreign nationals in the conflict.

Zimbabwe.headline: RBZ urges dealers to sell fuel in ZiG
Zimbabwe.detail: The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) is encouraging fuel dealers to transact in the local currency, ZiG, to promote its use and stability in the market, according to NewsDay.

Zimbabwe.headline: Funeral visit turns into nightmare as man loses car
Zimbabwe.detail: Bulawayo24 reports that a man attending a funeral had his car stolen, turning a sorrowful occasion into a personal crisis.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zim shines at ITB Berlin 2026, bags two awards
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe received two accolades at the Pacific Area Travel Writers Association (PATWA) Awards held during ITB Berlin 2026, a recognition of its tourism potential.

Zimbabwe.headline: Minister decries rise in irregular child migration
Zimbabwe.detail: NewsDay reports that a government minister has expressed concern over the increasing number of children migrating irregularly, highlighting the vulnerability of minors to trafficking and exploitation.
